content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

SUPERSEDED
by 1821 SLC(4)

To : Advice File: Section 82015

Date : 8/19/86

From : Jeanne Pritchard
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject : Advice Provided to Jacques O'Connell,
Lieutenant Governor's Office

Mr. O'Connell asked whether costs in connection with the
production and mailing of a brochure which contains references
to the Lieutenant Governor and a letter from the Lieutenant
Governor are in-kind contributions. The mailing is being
sent by the Indo-American Association for the purpose of promoting
a trade mission. The Indo-American Association is paying for
the production, and the Small Business Administration is paying
for the postage. The mailing consists of approximately 6,000
to 8,000 pieces which will be sent to California residents.

Conclusion: The portion of the costs of sending the mailing
which are allocable to the letter from the Lieutenant Governor
and to those portions of the brochure which contain a reference
to the Lieutenant Governor are in-kind contributions from the
Indo-American Association to the Lieutenant Governor. These costs
include production costs and also postage.

The attached letter to Mike Roos explains the reasoning
for the interpretation that the costs for the mailing are in-kind
contributions.

With regard to the payment of postage by the Small Business
Administration, in most situations, any third party which makes a
payment for a mailing such as this one, is making a payment for
political purposes. However, in this situation, the Small Business
Administration is clearly paying for the postage as a part of its
mandate as a federal government agency to implement a federal
program. The payment for postage is made to further the goals of
the federal agency and, therefore, is not for "political purposes."

However, the Indo-American Association's use of the postage
provided to it by the Small Business Administration is for "political
purposes" and, therefore, is an in-kind contribution from the
Association.
